findings.title: Unchecked type assertion panics on wrong dynamic type
findings.description: v := i.(string) is a single-value type assertion that panics with 'interface conversion' whenever the caller passes a value whose dynamic type is not string, crashing the program on untrusted or unexpected input.
findings.suggestion: Use the comma-ok form, v, ok := i.(string), and return an error or a sensible default when ok is false instead of panicking.
